title: phegopteris bukoensis
intro: Phegopteris bukoensis, also known as Buko fern or Buko maiden hair fern, is a species of fern native to the Philippines. It is a medicinal plant used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds, which have been found to possess antioxidant, anti-diabetic, and anti-cancer properties, providing potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and preventing cell damage. In herbal preparations, Phegopteris bukoensis is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
